data.description:
# Reduce the import-cycle SCC (import-linter contract + inversion fix + facade-read reroute)

## Context
A verified AST+Tarjan analysis found a ~69-module strongly-connected import component spanning the
facade, `_cli`, `_commands`, `graph`, `signing`, and most of `llm`, held together by function-local
lazy imports. Layering is enforced only by docstrings, and every new subsystem joins the knot. Three
remediations (approved):

## What
1. **Import-linter CI contract** — freeze the current cross-package edge/cycle set as a grandfathered
   baseline and fail only NEW cross-package edges (strangler pattern, like the module-size gate). A
   CI-only gate (no human review gate). Use import-linter/grimp or a custom grimp-based baseline check.
2. **Fix the reducer layering inversion** — `reducer/_processors.py` imports UP into
   `_engine_support.resolver` (the pure replay layer reaching into a higher layer). Extract the shared
   id/alias resolution primitive into a LEAF module both depend on (down, not up).
3. **Route leaf reads through `rebar._reads`** instead of `import rebar` (the facade), removing a class
   of facade back-edges.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Import-linter (or grimp-based) contract in CI: passes on current state, fails a newly-introduced cross-package cycle (proven by a test/among the contract's own checks).
- [ ] `reducer` no longer imports `_engine_support`; the shared resolution lives in a leaf module; reducer/replay tests green.
- [ ] Leaf modules that imported the `rebar` facade only for reads use `rebar._reads`; no behavior change; suite green.
- [ ] `make lint` + full suite green; commit(s) carry `rebar-ticket:` trailers.
data.title: Reduce the import-cycle SCC (import-linter contract + inversion fix + facade-read reroute)
